devanagari: भागवतधर्म निरूपणम् उद्धवस्य बदरिकाश्रमगमनं च । श्रीउद्धव उवाच । सुदुश्चरां इमां मन्ये योगचर्यामनात्मनः । यथाञ्जसा पुमान् सिद्ध्येत् तन्...
english_devnagari: bhāgavatadharma nirūpaṇam uddhavasya badarikāśramagamanaṃ ca śrīuddhava uvāca suduścarāṃ imāṃ manye yogacaryāmanātmanaḥ yathāñjasā pumān siddhyet tanme brūhyañjasācyuta

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation:
Uddhava submitted:
O Imperishable Lord! It is my considered opinion that the course of yoga as enunciated by you is impracticable to those who have no control over their minds. Pray, be pleased to elucidate to me (in easily understandable terms) that spiritual path by following which an ordinary person will attain perfection without any difficulty.

devanagari: प्रायशः पुण्डरीकाक्ष युञ्जन्तो योगिनो मनः । विषीदन्त्यसमाधानान् मनोनिग्रहकर्शिताः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: prāyaśaḥ puṇḍarīkākṣa yuñjanto yogino manaḥ viṣīdantyasamādhānān manonigrahakarśitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb]
[nominative plural from √ kṛś ], [vocative plural from √ kṛś ]
[nominative plural from √ kṛś ], [vocative plural from √ kṛś ], [accusative plural from √ kṛś ]

translation: Aspirants after yoga who endeavour to concentrate their minds are subjected to great strains in controlling their mind, and feeling fatigued and frustrated in attaining mental serenity, they become despondent, O lotus-eyed God.

devanagari: अथात आनन्ददुघं पदाम्बुजं । हंसाः श्रयेरन्नरविन्दलोचन । सुखं नु विश्वेश्वर योगकर्मभि । स्त्वन्माययामी विहता न मानिनः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: athāta ānandadughaṃ padāmbujaṃ haṃsāḥ śrayerannaravindalocana sukhaṃ nu viśveśvara yogakarmabhi stvanmāyayāmī vihatā na māninaḥ
translation: Hence, O Lotus-eyed Lord of the universe, swans i.e. those who can discriminate between what is essential and unessential (i.e. your devotees) joyfully resort to your lotus-feet which yield nectarlike spiritual bliss but these arrogant followers of the karma and yoga path devoid of your Bhakti—do not resort to them as they are confounded by your Māyā.

devanagari: किं चित्रमच्युत तवैतदशेषबन्धो । दासेष्वनन्यशरणेषु यदात्मसात्त्वम् । योऽरोचयत् सह मृगैः स्वयमीश्वराणां । श्रीमत्किरीटतटपीडितपादपीठः ॥ ४ ...
english_devnagari: kiṃ citramacyuta tavaitadaśeṣabandho dāseṣvananyaśaraṇeṣu yadātmasāttvam yo śrīmatkirīṭataṭapīḍitapādapīṭhaḥ
translation: What wonder is there, O Imperishable Lord, that you abide by the will of your servants who have dedicated themselves exclusively to you? Even though your foot-stool gets polished with the crests of the glorious crowns of great rulers like god Brahmā and other rulers of the world (while they prostrated themselves at your feet), you took delight in the friendship of beasts (monkeys like Sugrīva, Hanumān), O friend of all living beings.

devanagari: तं त्वाखिलात्मदयितेश्वरमाश्रितानां । सर्वार्थदं स्वकृतविद् विसृजेत को नु । को वा भजेत् किमपि विस्मृतयेऽनु भूत्यै । किं वा भवेन्न तव पाद...
english_devnagari: taṃ tvākhilātmadayiteśvaramāśritānāṃ sarvārthadaṃ svakṛtavid visṛjeta ko nu ko vā bhajet kimapi vismṛtaye kiṃ vā bhavenna tava pādarajojuṣāṃ naḥ
translation: What person who is grateful and conscious of the gracious blessings bestowed by you upon your dependants (devotees who resort to you) can forsake you, the Inner Controller, beloved-most and Ruler of all beings? What person will accept something other than you which, though leading to good fortune, make us forget about you? (or who would worship some other god for securing great fortune or celestial world?) What object is inaccessible to us who devote ourselves to the dust of your feet?

devanagari: नैवोपयन्त्यपचितिं कवयस्तवेश । ब्रह्मायुषापि कृतमृद्धमुदः स्मरन्तः । योऽन्तर्बहिस्तनुभृतां अशुभं विधुन्वन् । आचार्यचैत्त्यवपुषा स्वगतिं ...
english_devnagari: naivopayantyapacitiṃ kavayastaveśa brahmāyuṣāpi kṛtamṛddhamudaḥ smarantaḥ yo ācāryacaittyavapuṣā svagatiṃ vyanakti

[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: O Ruler of the universe! Wise persons who gratefully remember your gracious favours and are full of spiritual bliss, realize that they cannot repay your obligations even during the longest span of life like that of god Brahmā; for you are present externally as the spiritual guide and internally as the Antaryāmin (the Inner Controller) and thus you dispel all inauspiciousness (sins, evil desires) of all creatures and manifest your true nature to them.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्युद्धवेनात्यनुरक्तचेतसा । पृष्टो जगत्क्रीडनकः स्वशक्तिभिः । गृहीतमूर्तित्रय ईश्वरेश्वरो । जगाद सप्रेममनोहरस्मितः ॥ ७ ...
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityuddhavenātyanuraktacetasā pṛṣṭo jagatkrīḍanakaḥ svaśaktibhiḥ gṛhītamūrtitraya īśvareśvaro jagāda sapremamanoharasmitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When respectfully inquired thus by Uddhava who was deeply affectionate to him at heart, Lord Kṛṣṇa whose sport is the evolution of the universe, and who, by his potencies viz. Sattva, Rajas and Tamas, has assumed three forms (viz. that of Viṣṇu, Brahmā and Śiva respectively) and is the Supreme Lord of the rulers of the universe, with a winsome smile replied
affectionately to him.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि मम धर्मान् सुमङ्गलान् । याञ्छ्रद्धयाऽचरन् मर्त्यो मृत्युं जयति दुर्जयम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca hanta te kathayiṣyāmi mama dharmān sumaṅgalān yāñchraddhayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord said:
It is with pleasure that I shall explain to you the course of righteous duties which are exceedingly auspicious and pleasing to me. By following them with devotion, a mortal being can over-come the unconquerable death (Saṃsāra).

devanagari: कुर्यात् सर्वाणि कर्माणि मदर्थं शनकैः स्मरन् । मय्यर्पितमनश्चित्तो मद्धर्मात्ममनोरतिः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: kuryāt sarvāṇi karmāṇi madarthaṃ śanakaiḥ smaran mayyarpitamanaścitto maddharmātmamanoratiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ ṛ ], [accusative single from √ ṛ ]

translation: Remembering me deligently, one should perform all his duties dedicating them to me. He should resign and concentrate his heart and mind to me and should find delight in doing duties pertaining to me.

devanagari: देशान् पुण्यान् आश्रयेत मद्‍भक्तैः साधुभिः श्रितान् । देवासुरमनुष्येषु मद्‍भक्ताचरितानि च ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: deśān puṇyān āśrayeta mad‍bhaktaiḥ sādhubhiḥ śritān devāsuramanuṣyeṣu mad‍bhaktācaritāni ca
translation: He should resort to the holy lands (and sacred places) frequented by my saintly devotees, and he should follow the course of conduct of my devotees whether they be gods, Asuras or human beings.

devanagari: पृथक् सत्रेण वा मह्यं पर्वयात्रामहोत्सवान् । कारयेद् ‍गीत नृत्याद्यैः महाराजविभूतिभिः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthak satreṇa vā mahyaṃ parvayātrāmahotsavān kārayed ‍gīta nṛtyādyaiḥ mahārājavibhūtibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]

translation: Either individually or in a group he should arrange to celebrate, on holy days (like Ekādaśī) pilgrimages, processions or great festivities accompanied with songs, dances and other royal arrangements worthy of great kings.

devanagari: मामेव सर्वभूतेषु बहिरन्तरपावृतम् । ईक्षेतात्मनि चात्मानं यथा खममलाशयः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: māmeva sarvabhūteṣu bahirantarapāvṛtam īkṣetātmani cātmānaṃ yathā khamamalāśayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: He should consider that he visualizes me only, manifested in all beings, both internally and externally, like the sky. With a pure, sinless heart he should see me within himself also.

devanagari: इति सर्वाणि भूतानि मद्‍भावेन महाद्युते । सभाजयन् मन्यमानो ज्ञानं केवलमाश्रितः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sarvāṇi bhūtāni mad‍bhāvena mahādyute sabhājayan manyamāno jñānaṃ kevalamāśritaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: In this way, O greatly enlightened Uddhava, he should establish himself in pure knowledge and should consider all created beings as being the God himself and respect them all. He who looks with, an equal eye (without making any difference in) Brāhmaṇas and low caste peoples, a robber and one devoted to Brāhmaṇas, the sun and the spark of fire, a cruel and merciful person, (such impartial person) is really the spiritually wise person.

devanagari: ब्राह्मणे पुल्कसे स्तेने ब्रह्मण्येऽर्के स्फुलिङ्गके । अक्रूरे क्रूरके चैव समदृक् पण्डितो मतः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇe pulkase stene brahmaṇye akrūre krūrake caiva samadṛk paṇḍito mataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ paṇḍ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ paṇḍ class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: In this way, O greatly enlightened Uddhava, he should establish himself in pure knowledge and should consider all created beings as being the God himself and respect them all. He who looks with, an equal eye (without making any difference in) Brāhmaṇas and low caste peoples, a robber and one devoted to Brāhmaṇas, the sun and the spark of fire, a cruel and merciful person, (such impartial person) is really the spiritually wise person.

devanagari: नरेष्वभीक्ष्णं मद्‍भावं पुंसो भावयतोऽचिरात् । स्पर्धासूयातिरस्काराः साहङ्कारा वियन्ति हि ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: nareṣvabhīkṣṇaṃ mad‍bhāvaṃ puṃso bhāvayato spardhāsūyātiraskārāḥ sāhaṅkārā viyanti hi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ bhū ], [ablative single from √ bhū ], [genitive single from √ bhū ]
[ablative single from √ bhū ], [genitive single from √ bhū ]
[nominative plural from √ vī class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ vī class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ vī class 2 verb]

translation: A person who invariably considers me to be present in all human beings, finds that before he is free from the (feeling of) inimical competition (with the equals), jealousy (against superiors) and contempt (towards the inferiors) along with his egotism.

devanagari: विसृज्य स्मयमानान् स्वान् दृशं व्रीडां च दैहिकीम् । प्रणमेद् दण्डवद् भूमौ अश्वचाण्डालगोखरम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: visṛjya smayamānān svān dṛśaṃ vrīḍāṃ ca daihikīm praṇamed daṇḍavad bhūmau aśvacāṇḍālagokharam
translation: Disregarding one’s friends and relatives who smile at him in ridicule and setting aside the viewpoint (about the superiority) of one’s person and giving up the sense of shame, should prostrate himself like a staff in bowing to all beings upto a dog, a Cāṇḍāla (a man of the lowest caste) a cow and a donkey.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Strangely enough Padaratnāvalī distinguishes between Brāhmaṇas and oṃers in the list and lays down that one should physically lay prostrate before Brāhmaṇas but in the case of Cāṇḍālas and others he should bow mentally only.
(brāhmaṇādīn prati bhūmau daṇḍavan naman anyāṃścāṇḍālādīn prati manasāsmaraṇam iti bhedaḥ /)

devanagari: यावर् सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्‍भावो नोपजायते । तावदेवमुपासीत वाङ्मनःकायवृत्तिभिः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvar sarveṣu bhūteṣu mad‍bhāvo nopajāyate tāvadevamupāsīta vāṅmanaḥkāyavṛttibhiḥ
translation: He should continue to propitiate me orally, mentally and physically in this manner, so long as the perception of my presence in all beings is not engendered in him.

devanagari: सर्वं ब्रह्मात्मकं तस्य विद्ययाऽत्ममनीषया । परिपश्यन् उपरमेत् सर्वतो मुक्तसंशयः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvaṃ brahmātmakaṃ tasya vidyayā paripaśyan uparamet sarvato muktasaṃśayaḥ
translation: To him everything appears as permeated with Brahman by virtue of his knowledge that God is everywhere. Realizing this clearly and with all his doubts clarified, he should cease to participate in Karmas (or follow the path of Karma).

devanagari: अयं हि सर्वकल्पानां सध्रीचीनो मतो मम । मद्‍भावः सर्वभूतेषु मनोवाक्कायवृत्तिभिः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ hi sarvakalpānāṃ sadhrīcīno mato mama mad‍bhāvaḥ sarvabhūteṣu manovākkāyavṛttibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: It is my considered opinion that out of all the pathways to God, to perceive my presence in all beings in one’s thought, word and deeds is the most efficacious one.

devanagari: न ह्यङ्गोपक्रमे ध्वंसो मद्धर्मस्योद्धवाण्वपि । मया व्यवसितः सम्यङ् निर्गुणत्वादनाशिषः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: na hyaṅgopakrame dhvaṃso maddharmasyoddhavāṇvapi mayā vyavasitaḥ samyaṅ nirguṇatvādanāśiṣaḥ
translation: Dear Uddhava! Even if merely a beginning is made, there is no danger of the slightest loss (of the exertions made) in the righteous course of conduct prescribed by me, as it is free from wishful expectation, and it has been so systematically ordained by me as it would not be affected by guṇas. (It is not the ordinary Dharma laid down by Smṛti-writerslike Manu).

devanagari: यो यो मयि परे धर्मः कल्प्यते निष्फलाय चेत् । तदायासो निरर्थः स्याद् ‍भयादेरिव सत्तम ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: yo yo mayi pare dharmaḥ kalpyate niṣphalāya cet tadāyāso nirarthaḥ syād ‍bhayāderiva sattama
translation: [2] Whatever fruitless exertion of a worldly nature such as fleeing out of fear, lamenting out of grief) etc., if dedicated to me who am of transcendental nature, attains the status of Dharma (as in the case of Kaṃsa’s fear, Gopī’s love, Śiśupāla’s hatred).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī Acts of devotion, worship, contemplation on other deities is a fruitless exertion.
(tadāyāsas tatra kriyamāṇa-prayatno nirarthaḥ syāt /).

devanagari: एषा बुद्धिमतां बुद्धिः मनीषा च मनीषिणाम् । यत्सत्यमनृतेनेह मर्त्येनाप्नोति मामृतम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣā buddhimatāṃ buddhiḥ manīṣā ca manīṣiṇām yatsatyamanṛteneha martyenāpnoti māmṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: That one should attain to the Real through the unreal (body) and reach me, the immortal eternal Lord, by means of this mortal coil—This is the most intelligent act of an intellectual and the highest sagacity of the wise.

devanagari: एष तेऽभिहितः कृत्स्नो ब्रह्मवादस्य सङ्ग्रहः । समासव्यासविधिना देवानामपि दुर्गमः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa te samāsavyāsavidhinā devānāmapi durgamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: What has been expounded to you up till now by me either in details or in brief, constitutes the complete epitome of the definitive teaching on Para Brahman. This is difficult to grasp even for gods (though they are sāttvic by nature).

devanagari: अभीक्ष्णशस्ते गदितं ज्ञानं विस्पष्टयुक्तिमत् । एतद् विज्ञाय मुच्येत पुरुषो नष्टसंशयः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: abhīkṣṇaśaste gaditaṃ jñānaṃ vispaṣṭayuktimat etad vijñāya mucyeta puruṣo naṣṭasaṃśayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ gad ], [accusative single from √ gad ]
[vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]

translation: This spiritual knowledge has been repeatedly imparted to you with clear logical reasoning. A person who digests this will get all his doubts resolved and will be liberated from Saṃṣāra.

devanagari: सुविविक्तं तव प्रश्नं मयैतदपि धारयेत् । सनातनं ब्रह्मगुह्यं परं ब्रह्माधिगच्छति ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: suviviktaṃ tava praśnaṃ mayaitadapi dhārayet sanātanaṃ brahmaguhyaṃ paraṃ brahmādhigacchati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ guh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ guh class 1 verb]

translation: He who will treasure up in his memory my lucid, well-reasoned exposition of your queries, will attain to the eternal Supreme Brahman which is the secret (knowledge) of the Vedas.

devanagari: य एतन्मम भक्तेषु सम्प्रदद्यात् सुपुष्कलम् । तस्याहं ब्रह्मदायस्य ददाम्यात्मानमात्मना ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etanmama bhakteṣu sampradadyāt supuṣkalam tasyāhaṃ brahmadāyasya dadāmyātmānamātmanā
translation: I, of my own accord, will reveal myself to the expositor of the Brahman who would clearly explain this knowledge to my devotees.

devanagari: य एतत् समधीयीत पवित्रं परमं शुचि । स पूयेताहरहर्मां ज्ञानदीपेन दर्शयन् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etat samadhīyīta pavitraṃ paramaṃ śuci sa pūyetāharaharmāṃ jñānadīpena darśayan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]
[nominative single from √ dṛś ], [vocative single from √ dṛś ]

translation: He who would loudly read out this sanctifying, supremely holy text (containing my teaching), will every day make others see me by the light of spiritual wisdom, and will himself get purified (by realizing me in his mind purified by Yoga[3]).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
yoga-pariśuddheṇa manasā sākṣāt-kurvanniti bhāvaḥ—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: य एतत् श्ररद्धया नित्यं अव्यग्रः श्रृणुयान्नरः । मयि भक्तिं परां कुर्वन् कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etat śraraddhayā nityaṃ avyagraḥ śrṛṇuyānnaraḥ mayi bhaktiṃ parāṃ kurvan karmabhirna sa badhyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: He who devoutly and with concentrated mind listens to this every day and develops supreme devotion unto me, is never bound by Karmas.

devanagari: अप्युद्धव त्वया ब्रह्म सखे समवधारितम् । अपि ते विगतो मोहः शोकश्चासौ मनोभवः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: apyuddhava tvayā brahma sakhe samavadhāritam api te vigato mohaḥ śokaścāsau manobhavaḥ
translation: O friend, have you fully realized the true nature of Brahman? Has your infatuation and the grief that tormented your heart disappeared, dear friend?

devanagari: नैतत्त्वया दाम्भिकाय नास्तिकाय शठाय च । अशुश्रूषोरभक्ताय दुर्विनीताय दीयताम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: naitattvayā dāmbhikāya nāstikāya śaṭhāya ca aśuśrūṣorabhaktāya durvinītāya dīyatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single], [imperative passive third single]

translation: This knowledge should not be imparted to a hypocrite, a nonbeliever, a cheat, an unwilling listener, a person who is not devoted to me or an ill-mannered fellow.

devanagari: एतैर्दोषैर्विहीनाय ब्रह्मण्याय प्रियाय च । साधवे शुचये ब्रूयाद् ‍भक्तिः स्यात् शूद्रयोषिताम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: etairdoṣairvihīnāya brahmaṇyāya priyāya ca sādhave śucaye brūyād ‍bhaktiḥ syāt śūdrayoṣitām
translation: You should expound this to persons who are free from the above-mentioned defects, are friendly to Brāhmaṇas and (reciprocally) liked by them. You should teach this to those who are pious, holy and to Śūdras and women if they are devoted.

devanagari: नैतद् विज्ञाय जिज्ञासोः ज्ञातव्यं अवशिष्यते । पीत्वा पीयूषममृतं पातव्यं नावशिष्यते ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: naitad vijñāya jijñāsoḥ jñātavyaṃ avaśiṣyate pītvā pīyūṣamamṛtaṃ pātavyaṃ nāvaśiṣyate

translation: Once this spiritual wisdom is properly imbibed, nothing remains to be known by the aspirant of (spiritual) knowledge, just as nothing remains worth drinking when once the exquisitely sweet nectar is sipped.

devanagari: ज्ञाने कर्मणि योगे च वार्तायां दण्डधारणे । यावानर्थो नृणां तात तावांस्तेऽहं चतुर्विधः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: jñāne karmaṇi yoge ca vārtāyāṃ daṇḍadhāraṇe yāvānartho nṛṇāṃ tāta tāvāṃste
translation:
(There may be many things worth knowing and means of achieving object in the case of non-devotees, but I am everything to my devotee. Hence you seek resort in me alone.)
Whatever objectives or purposes are accomplished by means of (spiritual)knowledge, performance of righteous duties, yogic practices, pursuit of commerce and agriculture, and wielding ruling power—that is all the main four objectives in life (or Puruṣārthas), I myself am to you[4].
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā attempts to explain how each of the four puruṣārthas are covered by the activities mentioned in this Śloka.

devanagari: मर्त्यो यदा त्यक्तसमस्तकर्मा । निवेदितात्मा विचिकीर्षितो मे । तदामृतत्वं प्रतिपद्यमानो । मयाऽत्मभूयाय च कल्पते वै ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: martyo yadā tyaktasamastakarmā niveditātmā vicikīrṣito me tadāmṛtatvaṃ pratipadyamāno mayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: When a mortal being has renounced all courses of actions (prescribed in Śāstras) and has dedicated his self to me, I select him as the recipient of my Special Grace. He then, attains immortality and certainly becomes eligible to be one with me sharing my divine powers of omnipresence, omnipotence, etc.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । स एवमादर्शितयोगमार्गः । तदोत्तमःश्लोकवचो निशम्य । बद्धाञ्जलिः प्रीत्युपरुद्धकण्ठो । न किञ्चिदूचेऽश्रुपरिप्लुताक्षः ॥ ३५...
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca sa evamādarśitayogamārgaḥ tadottamaḥślokavaco niśamya baddhāñjaliḥ prītyuparuddhakaṇṭho na kiñcidūce

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having heard the speech of the Lord of hallowing renown, Uddhava, to whom the path of Yoga (union with the Almighty Lord) was thus lucidly explained, folded his palms (in reverence) and with his eyes overflowing with tears of joy and throat choked with overwhelming affection, he was unable to express himself.

devanagari: विष्टभ्य चित्तं प्रणयावघूर्णं । धैर्येण राजन्बहुमन्यमानः । कृताञ्जलिः प्राह यदुप्रवीरं । शीर्ष्णा स्पृशंस्तच्चरणारविन्दम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṭabhya cittaṃ praṇayāvaghūrṇaṃ dhairyeṇa rājanbahumanyamānaḥ kṛtāñjaliḥ prāha yadupravīraṃ śīrṣṇā spṛśaṃstaccaraṇāravindam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ viṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ viṣ class 3 verb]
[vocative single from √ viṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ viṣ class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb]

translation: With courageous efforts, he controlled and stabilised his mind which was agitated with overwhelming affection. Feeling (with gratitude) how blessed he was (by the Lord), he folded his palms (to pay obeisance) and touching His lotus- feet with his head, he submitted to the most prominent hero among Yadus, as follows:

devanagari: श्रीउद्धव उवाच । विद्रावितो मोहमहान्धकारो । य आश्रितो मे तव सन्निधानात् । विभावसोः किं नु समीपगस्य । शीतं तमो भीः प्रभवन्त्यजाद्य ॥ ३७...
english_devnagari: śrīuddhava uvāca vidrāvito mohamahāndhakāro ya āśrito me tava sannidhānāt vibhāvasoḥ kiṃ nu samīpagasya śītaṃ tamo bhīḥ prabhavantyajādya

[nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

translation: “The pitchy darkness of delusion clasped by me so far, has been dispelled by your mere enlightening presence (what need be said about your illuminating discourses?) O creator of god Brahmā, can cold, darkness or fear (of any of these) overpower a person who is sitting adjacent to the fire (or the sun).

devanagari: प्रत्यर्पितो मे भवतानुकम्पिना । भृत्याय विज्ञानमयः प्रदीपः । हित्वा कृतज्ञस्तव पादमूलं । कोऽन्यत् समीयाच्छरणं त्वदीयम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: pratyarpito me bhavatānukampinā bhṛtyāya vijñānamayaḥ pradīpaḥ hitvā kṛtajñastava pādamūlaṃ ko
translation: (Your compassion is limitless). Compassionate as you are, the brilliant lamp of spiritual wisdom has been given to me again. What grateful person would like to abandon the soles of your feet and resort to others for shelter.

devanagari: वृक्णश्च मे सुदृढः स्नेहपाशो । दाशार्हवृष्ण्यन्धकसात्वतेषु । प्रसारितः सृष्टिविवृद्धये त्वया । स्वमायया ह्यात्मसुबोधहेतिना ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛkṇaśca me sudṛḍhaḥ snehapāśo dāśārhavṛṣṇyandhakasātvateṣu prasāritaḥ sṛṣṭivivṛddhaye tvayā svamāyayā hyātmasubodhahetinā
translation: The strong bonds of (the net of) my affection for (my clansmen like) Dāśārhas, Vṛṣṇis, Andhakas and Sāttvatas which were extended by you through (the agency of) your Māyā, for the expansion (procreation) of our race, have been cut asunder by you with the weapon of self-realization.

devanagari: नमोऽस्तु ते महायोगिन् प्रपन्नमनुशाधि माम् । यथा त्वच्चरणाम्भोजे रतिः स्यादनपायिनी ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: namo yathā tvaccaraṇāmbhoje ratiḥ syādanapāyinī
translation: Obeisance to you, great yogin. I have approached you for protection. Be pleased to so guide me as I should find incessant joy from and affection for your lotus-feet.”

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । गच्छोद्धव मयाऽदिष्टो बदर्याख्यं ममाश्रमम् । तत्र मत्पादतीर्थोदे स्नानोपस्पर्शनैः शुचिः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca gacchoddhava mayā tatra matpādatīrthode snānopasparśanaiḥ śuciḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord Commanded:
Dear Uddhava! In compliance of my command, do you go to my hermitage called Badarī. Absolve yourself of all impurities there, by performing ablutions and sipping the holy waters of the sacred Gaṅgā which became sanctified by contact with my feet. Having shaken off all your sins and impurities by the very sight of the Alakanandā[5] put on bark garments and subsisting on forest-products (like fruits, roots), stay on unaffected by any longing for pleasures and comforts, my dear.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
A tributary of the Gaṅgā. Its source is traced by Captain Raper a little way beyond Badrīnāth at a waterfall called Vasudhārā (Skanda P. Viṣṇu Khaṇḍa III.6).—GADMI, P.3.

devanagari: ईक्षयालकनन्दाया विधूताशेषकल्मषः । वसानो वल्कलान्यङ्ग वन्यभुक्सुखनिःस्पृहः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: īkṣayālakanandāyā vidhūtāśeṣakalmaṣaḥ vasāno valkalānyaṅga vanyabhuksukhaniḥspṛhaḥ
translation:
The Lord Commanded:
Dear Uddhava! In compliance of my command, do you go to my hermitage called Badarī. Absolve yourself of all impurities there, by performing ablutions and sipping the holy waters of the sacred Gaṅgā which became sanctified by contact with my feet. Having shaken off all your sins and impurities by the very sight of the Alakanandā[5] put on bark garments and subsisting on forest-products (like fruits, roots), stay on unaffected by any longing for pleasures and comforts, my dear.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
A tributary of the Gaṅgā. Its source is traced by Captain Raper a little way beyond Badrīnāth at a waterfall called Vasudhārā (Skanda P. Viṣṇu Khaṇḍa III.6).—GADMI, P.3.

devanagari: तितिक्षुः द्वन्द्वमात्राणां सुशीलः संयतेन्द्रियः । शान्तः समाहितधिया ज्ञानविज्ञानसंयुतः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: titikṣuḥ dvandvamātrāṇāṃ suśīlaḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ śāntaḥ samāhitadhiyā jñānavijñānasaṃyutaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: Forbearing all experiences of opposite states (like heat and cold, pleasure and pain) continue to be straightforward and good in your behaviour. With your senses under control, serene and with your intellect (mind) concentrated on your Ātman, remain endowed with spiritual knowledge and self-realization. Pondering deeply and constantly and realizing whatever you have so assiduously learnt from me, you dedicate your mind and speech to me and continue to constantly abide by the duties prescribed by me. (If you do so) you will transcend the three courses (of existence caused by three guṇas—Saliva, Rajas and Tamas, viz. birth in the heaven and on the earth as a human being or a sub-human being or the three states of wakefulness, dreaming and deep sleep) and shall attain to me (who am beyond Māyā),

devanagari: मत्तोऽनुशिक्षितं यत्ते विविक्तमनुभावयन् । मय्यावेशितवाक्चित्तो मद्धर्मनिरतो भव । अतिव्रज्य गतीस्तिस्रो मामेष्यसि ततः परम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: matto mayyāveśitavākcitto maddharmanirato bhava ativrajya gatīstisro māmeṣyasi tataḥ param

translation: Forbearing all experiences of opposite states (like heat and cold, pleasure and pain) continue to be straightforward and good in your behaviour. With your senses under control, serene and with your intellect (mind) concentrated on your Ātman, remain endowed with spiritual knowledge and self-realization. Pondering deeply and constantly and realizing whatever you have so assiduously learnt from me, you dedicate your mind and speech to me and continue to constantly abide by the duties prescribed by me. (If you do so) you will transcend the three courses (of existence caused by three guṇas—Saliva, Rajas and Tamas, viz. birth in the heaven and on the earth as a human being or a sub-human being or the three states of wakefulness, dreaming and deep sleep) and shall attain to me (who am beyond Māyā),

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । स एवमुक्तो हरिमेधसोद्धवः । प्रदक्षिणं तं परिसृत्य पादयोः । शिरो निधायाश्रुकलाभिरार्द्रधीः । न्यषिञ्चदद्वन्द्वपरोऽप्यपक्...

[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka narrated:
Thus commanded by Lord Kṛṣṇa (devotion to whom terminates Saṃsāra), Uddhava circumambulated him by the right and placed his head on his feet. And even though he transcended the pairs of opposites like joy and sorrow, his heart melted (at the juncture of departure) and he moistened his feet with tears.

devanagari: सुदुस्त्यजस्नेहवियोगकातरो । न शक्नुवंस्तं परिहातुमातुरः । कृच्छ्रं ययौ मूर्धनि भर्तृपादुके । बिभ्रन् नमस्कृत्य ययौ पुनः पुनः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: sudustyajasnehaviyogakātaro na śaknuvaṃstaṃ parihātumāturaḥ kṛcchraṃ yayau mūrdhani bhartṛpāduke bibhran namaskṛtya yayau punaḥ punaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śak class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ śak class 5 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Nervous and afraid at the imminent separation from the Lord whose affection was extremely difficult to give up, and too much grieved to be able to leave him, Uddhava was deeply agonised. Bearing on his head the wooden sandals (given out of grace as a parting gift) of Lord Kṛṣṇa, Uddhava bowed to him again and again and proceeded[6] to Badarikāśrama.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
In order to reconcile this with Skandha III where Maitreya and Uddhava were given spiritual guidance by Kṛṣṇa, Bhāvāratha Dīpikā states that while on his way to Badarī, Uddhava heard of Kṛṣṇa’s going to Prabhāsa. He hastily returned and found him sitting alone. Maitreya also came there. Kṛṣṇa gave them spiritual guidance. But Skandha XI is silent about Uddhava’s return.

devanagari: ततस्तमन्तर्हुदि सन्निवेश्य । गतो महाभागवतो विशालाम् । यथोपदिष्टां जगदेकबन्धुना । तपः समास्थाय हरेरगाद् ‍गतिम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatastamantarhudi sanniveśya gato mahābhāgavato viśālām yathopadiṣṭāṃ jagadekabandhunā tapaḥ samāsthāya hareragād ‍gatim
translation:
Firmly installing him on the throne of his inmost heart, the great devotee of the Lord reached Viśālā (i.e. Badarikāśrama). Scrupulously following the course of (devotional) duties taught by Kṛṣṇa, the one Friend of the world, Uddhava ultimately attained to his state.
[The 2nd line in Padaratnāvalī’s Text:]
47(A). Having practised penance as instructed by the (only- real) Friend of the universe, he attained to Vaikuṇṭha, the region of Hari.

devanagari: य एतद् आनन्दसमुद्रसम्भृतं । ज्ञानामृतं भागवताय भाषितम् । कृष्णेन योगेश्वरसेविताङ्घ्रिणा । सच्छ्रद्धयाऽसेव्य जगद्विमुच्यते ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etad ānandasamudrasambhṛtaṃ jñānāmṛtaṃ bhāgavatāya bhāṣitam kṛṣṇena yogeśvarasevitāṅghriṇā sacchraddhayā
translation: Whosoever tastes with deep reverence ever so little of this nectar of spiritual knowledge overfull with the ocean of spiritual bliss, taught to his devotee Uddhava by Lord Kṛṣṇa, whose feet are resorted to and served by the masters of Yoga—he gets Final Emancipation from Saṃsāra along with the world, (the people) who associated themselves with him.

devanagari: भवभयमपहन्तुं ज्ञानविज्ञानसारं । निगमकृद् उपजह्रे भृङ्गवद् वेदसारम् । अमृतमुदधितश्चा पाययद् भृत्यवर्गान् । पुरुषं ऋषभमाद्यं कृष्णसंज्ञं...
english_devnagari: bhavabhayamapahantuṃ jñānavijñānasāraṃ nigamakṛd upajahre bhṛṅgavad vedasāram amṛtamudadhitaścā pāyayad bhṛtyavargān puruṣaṃ ṛṣabhamādyaṃ kṛṣṇasaṃjñaṃ nato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ pā ], [vocative single from √ pā ], [accusative single from √ pā ]
[nominative single from √ ad ], [accusative single from √ ad ]

translation:
I bow to the Supreme Person, designated as Kṛṣṇa, the First cause of the universe, the Author of the Vedas, who in order to terminate the Saṃsāra (transmigration of the Soul) of his devotees following the path of renunciation (nīvṛtti [nivṛtti]), and to dispel the fear of old age, death, etc. of his servants following the path of Pravṛtti, extracted like bee the (honey-like) essence of the Vedas in the form of spiritual knowledge and self-realization and gave it to his devotees (following Nivṛtti Marga) and offered the immortalizing nectar churned out of the sea to his servants (such as gods for drinking).
